result:
No `.h` file in the repo mentions `eloop.h` at all — confirmed with zero matches for any `#include ... eloop.h` (or even a bare grep for the string `eloop.h`) across every header file.

## Transitive-closure check

Checked every `.h` file in the repo for any `#include ... eloop.h`, and separately for any bare occurrence of the string `eloop.h` — **zero matches**. No header re-exports `eloop.h`, so there is no indirect-inclusion path at all.

**Conclusion: the count matches trivially** — since no header includes `eloop.h`, the transitive closure (`.c` files reaching `eloop.h` via some intermediate header) is the empty set, and the full set of `.c` files that ever pull in `eloop.h` (directly or indirectly) is exactly the same 117 files found by direct grep. No LSP-visible symbol lookup applies here since this is a preprocessor/textual question (per the skill's own note that `#include` graphs have no LSP primitive); this answer is grep-only, cross-checked with a second broader pattern to rule out missed spellings.
